item.title: Dominion Brothers ready for Samoa 7s outing
item.description: The TISA Insurance Dominion Brothers have completed their final preparations in Samoa for the Vailima Marist 7s and are now focused on their matches scheduled for tomorrow and Saturday. The side wrapped up its captain’s run this morning, marking the final training session before the start of competition.
item.content:encoded:
The TISA Insurance Dominion Brothers have completed their final preparations in Samoa for the Vailima Marist 7s and are now focused on their matches scheduled for tomorrow and Saturday.

The side wrapped up its captain’s run this morning, marking the final training session before the start of competition.

Fiji Airways Fiji men’s sevens half-back Filipo Bukayaro is leading the team in their campaign there.

It also presents another opportunity for the club to test itself against quality opposition while also providing valuable development opportunities for its younger players.

With a number of young players included in the travelling party, the tournament offers a chance for emerging talent to gain exposure to a higher level of competition in a different rugby environment.

For many of the younger members of the squad, the tour represents an opportunity to develop both on and off the field.

Exposure to overseas competition can help players adapt to different playing styles, improve their game management, and gain confidence against unfamiliar opponents.

Dominion has established itself as one of Fiji’s competitive club rugby teams and continue to invest in developing the next generation of players.

The club’s participation in regional tournaments provides an avenue for young athletes to showcase their abilities while learning from senior teammates and coaching staff.

The team has spent the past few days settling into Samoa and fine-tuning preparations ahead of the opening matches.

With the captain’s run now complete, the focus shifts to execution on game day as the squad aims to produce strong performances throughout the tournament.

As competition gets underway, Dominion Brothers will be looking to make the most of the experience, with the tour serving not only as a chance to compete for results but also as an opportunity to strengthen the squad for future campaigns.

The club will be hoping its blend of youth and experience can deliver positive performances when action begins this weekend.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Salawa’s hard work rewarded
item.description: THE sacrifices, hard work and dedication of Swire Shipping Fijian Drua flanker Kitione Salawa were recognised on Tuesday night when he walked away with the two biggest men’s awards at the club’s annual awards ceremony held at the Sofitel Fiji Resort and Spa. The powerful loose forward from Namotomoto, Nadi claimed the Enore Players’ Player of the Year Award and the prestigious McDonald’s Fiji Men’
item.content:encoded:
THE sacrifices, hard work and dedication of Swire Shipping Fijian Drua flanker Kitione Salawa were recognised on Tuesday night when he walked away with the two biggest men’s awards at the club’s annual awards ceremony held at the Sofitel Fiji Resort and Spa.

The powerful loose forward from Namotomoto, Nadi claimed the Enore Players’ Player of the Year Award and the prestigious McDonald’s Fiji Men’s Player of the Year Award, capping off what has been an outstanding season in Drua colours.

For Salawa, the achievement was the culmination of years of commitment and perseverance.

“Firstly, I want to thank God for the talent and strength that I was able to play with this season,” Salawa said.

“I also thank my wife and children for their support towards me during this season.

“I have been waiting for this award for a long time and I’m happy to receive it.”

The Enore Players’ Player of the Year Award carries special significance as it is voted by fellow teammates after every match throughout the season. It reflects the respect and admiration Salawa has earned from those who share the field and dressing room with him.

His second accolade, the McDonald’s Fiji Men’s Player of the Year Award, is selected by the men’s head coach and is regarded as the highest individual honour within the team.

Salawa’s performances throughout the season made him a deserving recipient. Week after week, he led from the front with his relentless work rate, physicality and consistency. Whether carrying the ball into heavy traffic, making crucial tackles or competing fiercely at the breakdown, the 25-year-old became one of the Drua’s most dependable performers.

His commitment has also earned the praise of coaches, management, sponsors and supporters who have watched him grow into one of the club’s key leaders.

From the rugby fields of Namotomoto to becoming one of the standout stars of the Drua, Salawa’s journey is a testament to perseverance, humility and hard work.

While the awards celebrate his achievements this season, Salawa remains grounded, choosing instead to share the honour with those who have supported him throughout his journey.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Hughes living his basketball dream
item.description: AT just 21 years old, Fiji basketball representative Keenan Hughes is already living the dream he once watched his parents achieve on the court. Raised in Suva and shaped by a strong basketball background, Hughes has emerged as one of Fiji’s exciting young talents, bringing overseas experience, leadership and ambition home for his first-ever Vodafone Fiji Cup appearance next month.
item.content:encoded:
AT just 21 years old, Fiji basketball representative Keenan Hughes is already living the dream he once watched his parents achieve on the court.

Raised in Suva and shaped by a strong basketball background, Hughes has emerged as one of Fiji’s exciting young talents, bringing overseas experience, leadership and ambition home for his first-ever Vodafone Fiji Cup appearance next month.

Hughes will be leading the Zodiac Rams Basketball Club for this year’s Vodafone Basketball Fiji Cup Championship.

The former Yat-Sen student began playing basketball at a very young age before taking the sport competitively in Class Five.

“I’ve just always been around the game,” Hughes said.

Growing up in a basketball household, the sport quickly became part of everyday life.

His father, Earl Hughes, represented Fiji, while his mother also played the game, inspiring his passion from an early age.

“My dad is a big reason why I play basketball today,” he said.

“There’s not a single thing I do on the floor that’s not criticised to some extent, but it’s always in a good way. They’ve always wanted me to make the most of my opportunities on and off the court.”

Hughes attended Yat-Sen Primary School before continuing at Yat-Sen Secondary School, where he further developed his game.

His journey took a major turn in 2021 during the COVID-19 pandemic when he moved alone to the United States on a basketball scholarship.

“That was one of the biggest challenges,” he admitted.

“I didn’t really get proper closure with people because of the restrictions. I love home, so a big part of me stayed in Fiji.”

Despite the emotional challenge, Hughes used the experience to grow both on and off the court while balancing life as a student-athlete in the US for nearly six years.

Now back home for the Vodafone Fiji Cup, Hughes is relishing the chance to reunite with childhood teammates and friends.

“It feels almost full circle getting to play with all of them again,” he said.

The Vodafone Basketball Fiji Cup starts today at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: 4 debutants named
item.description: THE McDonald’s Fijian Drua Women will begin their 2026 Super Rugby Women’s campaign this Saturday in Sydney, with head coach Mike Legge naming an exciting side to face defending champions the NSW Waratahs. Making their official Drua debuts at Leichhardt Oval are Vodafone Fijiana 15s representatives Varanisese Qoro and Ivamere Rokowati, along with Nadroga lock Karavaki Lutumaibau and Nasinu standou
item.content:encoded:
THE McDonald’s Fijian Drua Women will begin their 2026 Super Rugby Women’s campaign this Saturday in Sydney, with head coach Mike Legge naming an exciting side to face defending champions the NSW Waratahs.

Making their official Drua debuts at Leichhardt Oval are Vodafone Fijiana 15s representatives Varanisese Qoro and Ivamere Rokowati, along with Nadroga lock Karavaki Lutumaibau and Nasinu standout Aqela Raitubu. The quartet joins a starting lineup packed with international experience.

Several standout performers from the club’s pre-season campaign have also earned selection. Props Zipporah Sorokacika and Keri Lawavou, Nadroga’s Vilisi Tivalele, former Kadavu captain Vatiseva Cavuru, Ruth Raketekete and Michella’e Stolz have all been rewarded for their impressive trial performances with places in the match-day squad.

The starting 15 sees the most capped Drua player of all-time (25 appearances) Karalaini Naisewa (1) at loosehead prop, with Tiana Robanakadavu (3) at tighthead. Captain Bitila Tawake (2) will start at hooker.

In the middle row, Vunamoli, Nadi native Mereoni Nakesa (4) partners the debuting Karavaki Lutumaibau (5). The squad’s youngest member, 18-year-old Carletta Yee (6) lines up at blindside flanker with Nunia Daunimoala (7) at openside. Vice-captain Alfreda Fisher (8) starts the match at number 8. In the backline, the experienced Evivi Senikarivi (9) gets the nod to start at halfback, partnering Varanisese Qoro (10) who is at flyhalf. Flyer Aqela Raitubu (11) starts on the left wing with vice-captain Kolora Lomani (14) on the right wing. Josivini Naihamu (12) starts the match at inside centre with Ivamere Rokowati (13) at outside centre. Litiana Vueti (15) has been handed the fullback duties for the match.

For impact this week, Keleni Marawa (16) is backup hooker, Zipporah Sorokacika (17) is loosehead, with Keri Lawavou (18) covering the tighthead prop position. Lanky second rower Vilisi Tivalele (19) will cover for the locks and Salaseini Railumu (20) backs up the loose forward trio. Vatiseva Cavuru (21) will cover the halves with Ruth Raketekete (22) covering the centres. Michella’e Stolz (23) completes matchday squad covering the back three.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Drua Women eye strong start against Waratahs
item.description: THE McDonald’s Fijian Drua Women are eager to begin their 2026 Super W campaign on a high note when they take on defending champions the NSW Waratahs Women in Sydney this Saturday. Head coach Mike Legge says the team has focused heavily on discipline and skill execution in the lead-up to their season opener after two valuable pre-season matches against the Waratahs and the Queensland Reds Women.
item.content:encoded:
THE McDonald’s Fijian Drua Women are eager to begin their 2026 Super W campaign on a high note when they take on defending champions the NSW Waratahs Women in Sydney this Saturday.

Head coach Mike Legge says the team has focused heavily on discipline and skill execution in the lead-up to their season opener after two valuable pre-season matches against the Waratahs and the Queensland Reds Women.

“Discipline is a big work-on for us from both games, and just our skill execution.

“That’s been a big focus for us this week and last week. If we can implement what we’re doing here at our training base and translate that onto the field on Saturday, it should lead to a good performance,” Legge said.

The Drua head into the clash with confidence after encouraging performances during their trial matches, with Legge pleased by the energy and attitude shown by the squad.

“The spirits are really high. We had a good couple of sessions yesterday and there was a lot of energy in our afternoon session.

“The girls have reacted really well and they’re just ready to play. They’re ready to get stuck in and start the competition on Saturday.”

While the Swire Shipping Fijian Drua men’s side narrowly missed out on the Super Rugby Pacific playoffs, Legge said the women’s squad remains solely focused on its own ambitions.

“The boys and their results don’t really come into calculations for us. It’s all about what we can achieve.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Basketball faces venue struggles
item.description: BASKETBALL Fiji interim chief operating officer William Peters says securing venues remains one of the biggest challenges facing the sport despite continued growth in local competitions. Peters revealed that venue availability has been an ongoing issue over the years, forcing the organisation into repeated negotiations to confirm courts for major tournaments.
item.content:encoded:
BASKETBALL Fiji interim chief operating officer William Peters says securing venues remains one of the biggest challenges facing the sport despite continued growth in local competitions.

Peters revealed that venue availability has been an ongoing issue over the years, forcing the organisation into repeated negotiations to confirm courts for major tournaments.

“We’re working with Fiji Sports Council to be able to book down our events well in advance so we’re not running around or having to have negotiations with them every so often,” Peters said.

Despite the challenge, Basketball Fiji has managed to secure venues for two of its biggest tournaments this year — the secondary schools’ championship and the Vodafone Fiji Cup competition.

Peters said resolving venue uncertainty is a major priority because it directly impacts planning, logistics and the overall quality of competitions.

The Vodafone Fiji Cup competition will feature 16 teams, with eight men’s and eight women’s sides representing the four major associations around the country — Raiwaqa, Central, Nadi and Lautoka.

Peters added that western teams are preparing strongly for the tournament and are expected to bring fierce competition for the title.

He said Basketball Fiji hopes to continue building the event in the coming years as the sport gains momentum around the country.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Raiwaqa targets title defence
item.description: RETAINING the title is Raiwaqa Basketball Club’s sole objective going into the Vodafone Basketball Fiji Cup Championships. The 2026 Vodafone Basketball Fiji Cup Championships is taking place from today until Saturday at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva.
item.content:encoded:
RETAINING the title is Raiwaqa Basketball Club’s sole objective going into the Vodafone Basketball Fiji Cup Championships.

The 2026 Vodafone Basketball Fiji Cup Championships is taking place from today until Saturday at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva.

Club veteran and pioneer Waisea Uluivanuavatu says preparations have been encouraging as the club’s teams continue to fine-tune their combinations ahead of the three-day tournament which starts today.

The Raiwaqa club will be represented by defending men’s champions Boston, women’s defending champion Dynamic Redz, development team Mighty Mights and Iconic Redz.

Uluivanuavatu said players had shown commitment throughout their build-up despite balancing work, studies and family responsibilities.

“From what I have seen, the players are well prepared and they are making every effort to attend training,” he said.

“We have been focusing on combinations, defence and most importantly improving our basketball IQ.”

Boston and Dynamic Redz enters the tournament as the defending champion after claiming last year’s title and Uluivanuavatu said expectations remained high.

“As always, when we enter any tournament, we want to win. We want to retain the title.”

He said Raiwaqa’s strength had always been its mental toughness and aggressive style of play, qualities that had become synonymous with the club over the years.

“Raiwaqa is known for being mentally tough and aggressive, but we also need to continue improving our basketball IQ because the game is becoming smarter and more competitive.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Daunivavana eyes Australasian belt
item.description: BOXER Ratu Rakuro Daunivavana will face one of the toughest tests of his professional career when he takes on Brisbane’s highly regarded rising contender Yungubaddi Yanner for the Australasian Welterweight Title in Hamilton, Queensland this Saturday. The title presents a major opportunity for Daunivavana to claim regional honours and further elevate Fiji’s presence on the international boxing stag
item.content:encoded:
BOXER Ratu Rakuro Daunivavana will face one of the toughest tests of his professional career when he takes on Brisbane’s highly regarded rising contender Yungubaddi Yanner for the Australasian Welterweight Title in Hamilton, Queensland this Saturday.

The title presents a major opportunity for Daunivavana to claim regional honours and further elevate Fiji’s presence on the international boxing stage.

Daunivavana heads into the bout with considerable experience, having built a reputation as one of Fiji’s most active and resilient professional fighters.

Throughout his career, he has faced several quality opponents and has consistently shown a willingness to take on difficult challenges.

Among his notable performances are victories over experienced boxers Ronald Naidu and Sireli Navua. Daunivavana also demonstrated his knockout power with a second-round technical knockout win over Masitogi Rokotuva, proving he can finish fights when opportunities arise.

His combination of experience, toughness and ring awareness is expected to be key against Yanner, who is regarded as one of Australia’s emerging prospects in the welterweight division.

Daunivavana’s preparation has focused heavily on fitness, speed and executing a disciplined game plan.

His camp believes those factors will play an important role against a younger opponent who is expected to use his speed and aggressive style throughout the contest.

Head coach Osea Nanovu remains confident that Daunivavana remains confident that Rakuro has done the necessary work to emerge victorious.

“Rakuro will win the title,” Nanovu said.

Daunivavana is equally confident ahead of the championship bout and believes the preparation his team has put in will pay off in his fight.

According to a video shared on the team’s Facebook story, Daunivavana expressed confidence in his preparation ahead of the title bout.

“We have come prepared,” Daunivavana said.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
